description: A rigid container shaped by flat sides, usually with a distinct opening or lid. It may hold tools under a bench, letters on a shelf, or food during a journey; 'molu' (container) covers every receptacle, while 'molupa' adds panels and a shape that stays when empty.
sound_symbolism: Low nasal 'mo' begins behind closed lips. Stressed 'lu' releases the tongue into deep 'u'; across them, 'molu' (container) is audible in full. Final 'pa' brings the sound back to the lips and opens into broad 'a'. A rounded enclosure gives way to a wide final plane.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha molupa lo tenoa phelu.' (This box holds tools.) A surrounding phrase gives its material or lid. A box stays a box whether open or closed.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A fitted wooden box protected letters, seed, medicines, and edge tools from damp or careless handling.

tags.creation: a rigid container assembled from flat sides
tags.physical: a holder that retains its shape when empty
